note: Raised by user as 'downloads are fucked again' after an install appeared stuck at 90%. Investigation 2026-07-20 found F1 (failed download deletes its resumable partial - 5.66GB lost live on Chroma Flash), F2 (single-stream ~19MB/s against a ~40MB/s link), F3 (no speed/ETA in the UI at all), F4 (missed/stale completion notification - instrumented, unconfirmed). User's initial theory that the MPI-310 plugin agent broke it was disproven. The original 90% Install-chip symptom did not reproduce and stays unexplained.
Created directly in doing/investigating - investigation already done in-session, findings recorded on the card. No code fix shipped yet.

F1 shipped: resume-on-failure in routes/downloadManager.js - partials kept on failure/stall/shutdown, explicit Range resume via marker-blessed resumeFromFile, cancel still deletes, resumed streams fall back to disk-read SHA256. Orphaned spec test now passes; 8 adjacent test files green. Live Range-resume verification pending app restart.
F3 shipped: live speed + ETA row in the detail slide-over (template row above the actions footer, patched in place on progress ticks). Needs renderer reload to appear.

F2 retracted (SDXL hit 40.7 MB/s on the same single-stream path that gave Chroma ~19 MB/s - object-specific, not a TCP ceiling). F4 diagnosed from trace output: the dep-level complete clears state.downloadJobs 13s before the model-level complete arrives, so the notification path finds no job. Fix not yet written.

F1 live-verified: quit at 20%, partial+marker survived, resumed at 25% (1.41GB -> 3.18GB, never reset). Temp [trace] reverted. download() now logs resume-vs-clean-start. Cancel-deletes check still pending; F4 diagnosed but unfixed.

F5 recorded: resumed installs make _checkModelJobsComplete (legacy map, reads 'downloading') call _setModelStatus on a store job already settled 'done' -> transition correctly rejected. Benign, install completed fine, but it is the legacy-map vs installStore split MPI-276 was consolidating. Not pre-existing - surfaced by the F1 resume change.
